coverage: exact actor-day deduplication, catalog mapping, 64 immutable frames, verification before SQLite deletion
complete-partial-complete projection recovery and 30-bit coverage mask including D-29
Meilisearch pending-processing-failed-resubmit-succeeded task recovery without premature watermark or GC

meilisearch_ranking.expected_semantics: text relevance precedes heat for non-empty queries; empty queries are heat-first hot lists

limitations: Synthetic actor/hash stream with only 2000 distinct hashes; dictionary overhead is under-represented, so the S-005 key-shape benchmark remains the capacity authority.
Docker Desktop storage is not the production server filesystem and does not establish production fsync/checkpoint tail latency.
Resource polling was sampled and the run did not combine maximum metadata indexing, search traffic, day sealing and heat ingestion simultaneously.
This is an implementation and directional resource gate, not the user-authorized final stability test.
decision: Accept SQLite/WAL exact daily state and PostgreSQL daily frames without Redis as the required path; retain Redis only as an optional future intraday cache.
rollback: Disable Heat, preserve crawler spools and PostgreSQL frame/manifests, then replay after repair; do not delete a daily SQLite accumulator until its 64-frame manifest is durably verified.
